prerequisites: A Gong account with tech admin (administrator) access, since only admins can generate API credentials.
Access to the Gong Admin center under Settings > Ecosystem > API to create the access key and access key secret.
A value for the required credentials config field (either Access Key authentication or OAuth).
Optionally, a start_date in ISO-8601 format (for example 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z) to bound how far back data is pulled.
Optionally, a num_workers value (1 to 10, default 4) to control how many streams are synced in parallel.

authMethods.name: Access Key authentication (Basic auth)
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.access_key and credentials.access_key_secret secret fields. A Gong admin generates an access key and access key secret in the Admin center under Settings > Ecosystem > API. The access key is sent as the HTTP Basic auth username and the access key secret as the password.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0
authMethods.summary: Maps to the credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token secret fields. Authorized through Gong's OAuth flow; tokens are refreshed automatically.

setupSteps.title: Confirm Gong admin access
setupSteps.body: Sign in to Gong with an account that holds the tech admin (administrator) role. Only administrators can create API credentials.

setupSteps.title: Generate Access Key credentials
setupSteps.body: In the Gong Admin center, go to Settings > Ecosystem > API and click Get API Key to generate an access key and access key secret. Provide these as credentials.access_key and credentials.access_key_secret.

setupSteps.title: Or authenticate with OAuth
setupSteps.body: Instead of an access key, authorize through Gong's OAuth flow. This populates the credentials.client_secret, credentials.access_token, and credentials.refresh_token fields, and tokens are refreshed automatically.

setupSteps.title: Set the connector configuration
setupSteps.body: Supply the chosen authentication under the required credentials field. Optionally set start_date (ISO-8601, for example 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z) to control the earliest data fetched, and num_workers (1 to 10, default 4) to control how many streams are synced in parallel. The default of 4 is tuned to stay within Gong's limit of 3 calls per second.

sources.title: Receive access to the API - Gong Help Center
